payload.question: provide my Population, Sample and Sampling Techniques. write it the way it is on the document.
payload.answer:
**Population of the Study**
The target population for this study comprises all junior secondary school JSS teachers and all JSS students in the Ibadan metropolis, Oyo State, Nigeria. The Ibadan metropolis is geographically and administratively extensive, covering several Local Government Areas LGAs.
The specific target groups within this population are:
JSS Teachers: All teachers instructing core subjects (English Language, Mathematics, Basic Science, and Social Studies) at the JSS level. These subjects are selected because they represent the primary areas where content delivery is critical and where the use of code switching is most likely to be employed as a pedagogical strategy.
Based on the most recent estimates from the Oyo State Ministry of Education (as of the time of this proposal), the total population is estimated to be approximately 3,000 JSS teachers and over 60,000 JSS students across the Ibadan metropolis.

**Sample and Sampling Technique**
To ensure the selection of a sample that is both manageable and highly representative of the diverse socio educational landscape of Ibadan, a multi stage sampling technique was employed.
Stage 1: Selection of local governments LGAs, Four LGAs were selected from the Ibadan metropolis using purposive sampling. The selection ensured a mix of LGAs representing the core urban center (Ibadan North), the peri urban areas (Ibadan South West), and the more densely populated residential areas (Ibadan North East). This stratification ensures that the sample reflects the varying socio economic and educational environments within the city.
Stage 2: Selection of schools from the four selected LGAs, a total of 5 junior secondary schools were selected using a combination of stratified and simple random sampling. The schools were stratified into two groups: Public Schools and Private Schools. Three public schools and two private schools were randomly selected from the list of all JSS schools in the chosen LGAs. This stratification is crucial as public and private schools often differ significantly in their adherence to language policies and the linguistic background of their students.
Stage 3: Selection of Teachers and Students
Teachers: A total of 20 teachers were selected. Five teachers were randomly selected from the core subject teachers in each of the 5 selected schools using simple random sampling.
Students: A total of 80 students were selected. Twelve students were randomly selected from the JSS cass registers in each of the 5 selected schools using simple random sampling
The total sample size for the study was 1000 respondents (20 teachers and 80 students). This sample size is considered statistically adequate for generalising the findings to the target population (Nunnally 1978).
payload.original_text:
CHAPTER THREE
RESEARCH METHODOLOGY
Introduction
This chapter provides a detailed and comprehensive description of the research methodology employed to investigate the benefits and challenges of code-switching as a pedagogical tool in junior secondary schools in Ibadan. The chapter systematically covers the research design, the population and sampling procedures, the instrumentation, the procedures for establishing validity and reliability, the data collection process, and the statistical techniques utilized for data analysis.
Research Type
Population Sample and Sampling Techniques
Research Instrument
Procedure for Data Collection
Data Analysis Techniques
Research Type
The study adopted the descriptive survey design, this design is fundamentally concerned with describing the characteristics of a population or phenomenon accurately and systematically (Creswell 2014). It is the most appropriate design for this study because the primary objective is to ascertain the current state of affairs regarding the practice, functions, benefits, and challenges of code switching as perceived by teachers and students in the Ibadan junior secondary school context.
The descriptive survey design allows for the collection of quantitative data from a large, representative sample, which is essential for generalising the findings to the entire population of JSS teachers and students in the Ibadan metropolis. The design is non experimental, meaning no variables are manipulated or controlled; rather, the study focuses on describing the existing patterns of code-switching usage and the perceptions surrounding it. The use of a structured questionnaire facilitates the collection of standardized data necessary for statistical analysis.
Population of the Study
The target population for this study comprises all junior secondary school JSS teachers and all JSS students in the Ibadan metropolis, Oyo State, Nigeria. The Ibadan metropolis is geographically and administratively extensive, covering several Local Government Areas LGAs.
The specific target groups within this population are:
JSS Teachers: All teachers instructing core subjects (English Language, Mathematics, Basic Science, and Social Studies) at the JSS level. These subjects are selected because they represent the primary areas where content delivery is critical and where the use of code switching is most likely to be employed as a pedagogical strategy.
Based on the most recent estimates from the Oyo State Ministry of Education (as of the time of this proposal), the total population is estimated to be approximately 3,000 JSS teachers and over 60,000 JSS students across the Ibadan metropolis.
Sample and Sampling Technique
To ensure the selection of a sample that is both manageable and highly representative of the diverse socio educational landscape of Ibadan, a multi stage sampling technique was employed.
Stage 1: Selection of local governments LGAs, Four LGAs were selected from the Ibadan metropolis using purposive sampling. The selection ensured a mix of LGAs representing the core urban center (Ibadan North), the peri urban areas (Ibadan South West), and the more densely populated residential areas (Ibadan North East). This stratification ensures that the sample reflects the varying socio economic and educational environments within the city.
Stage 2: Selection of schools from the four selected LGAs, a total of 5 junior secondary schools were selected using a combination of stratified and simple random sampling. The schools were stratified into two groups: Public Schools and Private Schools. Three public schools and two private schools were randomly selected from the list of all JSS schools in the chosen LGAs. This stratification is crucial as public and private schools often differ significantly in their adherence to language policies and the linguistic background of their students.
Stage 3: Selection of Teachers and Students 
Teachers: A total of 20 teachers were selected. Five teachers were randomly selected from the core subject teachers in each of the 5 selected schools using simple random sampling.
Students: A total of 80 students were selected. Twelve students were randomly selected from the JSS cass registers in each of the 5 selected schools using simple random sampling 
The total sample size for the study was 1000 respondents (20 teachers and 80 students). This sample size is considered statistically adequate for generalising the findings to the target population (Nunnally 1978).
Research instruments
The primary instrument for data collection was a structured questionnaire Code-Switching as a Pedagogical Tool Questionnaire CSPTQ. The questionnaire was developed by the researcher based on the literature review and the research objectives. It was divided into two distinct versions to cater to the specific perspectives of the two respondent groups: Teacher Questionnaire on Code-Switching TQCS and Student Questionnaire on Code-Switching SQCS
Both versions of the questionnai

payload.question: provide my Population, Sample and Sampling Techniques
payload.answer:
**Population:**
The target population for this study comprises all junior secondary school (JSS) teachers and all JSS students in the Ibadan metropolis, Oyo State, Nigeria.
Specifically:
*   JSS Teachers: All teachers instructing core subjects (English Language, Mathematics, Basic Science, and Social Studies) at the JSS level. The total population is estimated to be approximately 3,000 JSS teachers.
*   JSS Students: All JSS students across the Ibadan metropolis. The total population is estimated to be over 60,000 JSS students.

**Sample:**
The total sample size for the study was 100 respondents, consisting of:
*   20 teachers
*   80 students

**Sampling Techniques:**
A multi-stage sampling technique was employed:
*   **Stage 1 (Selection of Local Government Areas - LGAs):** Four LGAs were selected from the Ibadan metropolis using **purposive sampling**. The selected LGAs were Ibadan North (core urban), Ibadan South West (peri-urban), and Ibadan North East (densely populated residential).
*   **Stage 2 (Selection of Schools):** From the four selected LGAs, a total of 5 junior secondary schools were selected using a combination of **stratified and simple random sampling**. The schools were stratified into two groups: Public Schools and Private Schools. Three public schools and two private schools were randomly selected.
*   **Stage 3 (Selection of Teachers and Students):**
    *   **Teachers:** A total of 20 teachers were selected. Five teachers were randomly selected from the core subject teachers in each of the 5 selected schools using **simple random sampling**.
    *   **Students:** A total of 80 students were selected. Twelve students were randomly selected from the JSS class registers in each of the 5 selected schools using **simple random sampling**.

وان كان من غير أهلها فانفعه واكبر دوائه افيون وملح
جزء يدقان ويشربان بالماء الحار ويستعمل بعد ذلك مسلوقة الضأن ويكون ذلك في
فصل الخريف فهو اكبر دوائه والله أعلم (بيت نسائه ومواصلاته) الحوت والمشترى
يتزوج من النساء بالزوج من اثنين الى اربعة الأولى تفارقه اما بموت أو بطلاق وربما
كانت الثانية معه الى الممات وربما تأكل ماله امرأة وتقهر وينال غرامة بسبب معاشرة
النساء والله أعلم (بيت خوفه وموته) الحمل والمريخ يخاف عليه من الموت اذا كان
طالع السنة بهما وان بدأ به مرض يوم الثلاثاء في شهر برمهات كان سببا لموته وذلك اذا
كان ابن سبع سنين أو أربعة عشر سنة أو ثمانية وعشرين سنة فان نجا من ذلك كله عاش
الى خمسة وسبعين سنة وقيل الى ثلاثة وثمانين سنة والله أعلم (بيت أسفاره وحركاته)
الثور والزهرة يكون أحسن سفره يوم الجمعة باكرا بالنهار الى القبلة والشرق خير له
وينال الزيارة الى بيت الله الحرام وينال أسفارا كثيرة وينال تعبا ومشقة في سفره ولا
يسافر في البر وحده ويخاف عليه من اللصوص ان يسرقوا منه شيئا وسفر البحر خير له
من البر والله أعلم (بيت عزه وسلطانه) الجوزاء وعطارد يكون له محبة ومودة عند
الاكابر والامراء والوزراء وأرباب الاقلام ويصحب رجلا جليل القدر وينال منه
معزة ورفعة وشأنا عظيما محبوبا عند الغرباء محمود الصحبة والاقامة ولا يصحبه أحد
الا أحبه وأعزه والله أعلم (بيت رجائه وآماله) السرطان والقمر يؤمل آمالا
كثيرة وينال ما يؤمله ويرخيه ولا يموت الا في سعادة دائمة ويرزق الدين والصلاح
والتوبة الخالصة وحسن اليقين ويرى أحلاما كثيرة بخير واذا عزم على أمر لم يرجع
عنه حتى يفعله والله أعلم (بيت أعدائه وحساده) الاسد والشمس يكون أعداؤه من
كل مملوك ومملوكة ويخاف عليه من رجل غليظ الجثة اصفر اللون يعانده ويسعى له في
الاذية والله تعالى ناصر عليهم محسود من أقاربه ما كول مذموم بين أصحابه يوافقه من
النساء والاصحاب من كان نجمه ترابيا كالجدي والسنبلة والثور وليحذر ممن كان نجمه

- 40 -
And if he is not of its people, then benefit him, and its greatest remedy is opium and salt, a portion to be pounded and drunk with hot water. After that, boiled lamb is used, and that should be in the autumn season, for it is its greatest remedy. And God knows best. (House of his women and his relationships) Pisces and Jupiter. He marries women, from two to four. The first one leaves him either by death or by divorce, and perhaps the second one stays with him until death. And perhaps a woman consumes his wealth and overpowers him, and he incurs a fine due to associating with women. And God knows best. (House of his fear and death) Aries and Mars. He is feared for death if the ascendant of the year is with them, and if an illness begins with him on a Tuesday in the month of Baramhat, it would be a cause for his death. And that is if he is seven years old, or fourteen years old, or twenty-eight years old. If he survives all of that, he lives until seventy-five years, and it is said until eighty-three years. And God knows best. (House of his travels and movements) Taurus and Venus. His best travel is on Friday early in the day towards the Qibla, and the East is better for him. And he attains a visit to the Sacred House of God, and he undertakes many travels, and he experiences fatigue and hardship in his travels. And he should not travel on land alone, and he is feared for thieves stealing something from him. And sea travel is better for him than land travel. And God knows best. (House of his glory and authority) Gemini and Mercury. He will have love and affection from dignitaries, princes, ministers, and men of letters. And he accompanies a man of great stature, and he gains from him honor, elevation, and great standing, beloved by strangers, praised in companionship and residence. And no one accompanies him except that they love him and honor him. And God knows best. (House of his hope and aspirations) Cancer and the Moon. He hopes for many aspirations, and he attains what he hopes for and finds ease in it. And he does not die except in perpetual happiness, and he is blessed with religion, righteousness, sincere repentance, and good certainty. And he sees many good dreams, and if he resolves upon a matter, he does not turn back from it until he accomplishes it. And God knows best. (House of his enemies and enviers) Leo and the Sun. His enemies will be from every male and female slave, and he is feared for a man of stout build, yellow in color, who opposes him and seeks to harm him. And God Almighty is his helper against them. Envied by his relatives, slandered and blamed among his companions. He is compatible with women and companions whose star is earthy, like Capricorn, Virgo, and Taurus. Factors that lead to Divorce. ①
end up in divorce

Dear Sir, 
 
LETTER OF APPEAL FOR MAKE- UP CONTINOUIS ASSESSMENT 
I am writing to formally apologize for my absence from the conducted continuous assessment 
(CA) tests for MCB 221 and BIO 203.   
My absence was due to unforeseen financial constraints which led to the late payment of my 
school fees. Consequently, I was unable to complete my registration in time to participate in the 
scheduled assessments.  
I humbly request your kind consideration to allow me to take a make-up test.   
Thank you for your time and kind consideration. I look forward to your favorable response.   
 
 
Yours Faithfully,   
 
 
  
OYINLOYE MERCY ISRAEL  
24/25PA098  
07026987194
